description: I created this as a Virtual Machine at my Organization and used it for years to be able to remote in from my computer and phone. As an IT Administrator this was super useful so I could monitor on-site equipment, run commands, etc. I've only stopped using it because my new Router has a built-in VPN connection App so I no longer needed OpenVPN, but I'd suggest it to anyone wanting one.
author: Glen N.
pros: It's a Linux Server that you setup and then you can remote into your things from off-site. It's Open Source and free to use.
cons: Nothing I can think of. Their documentation was sound enough I could follow along to set it up myself. Just don't expect expert support on free solutions, that's the trade off for getting things free.

review_title: Great option for having your own VPN
author: Manuel D.
pros: It is very reliable and fast to connect, easy to manage users and the stability is 10/10
cons: Since it requires your own server to deploy it mat be more expensive depending on the numbers of users

review_title: Access Server, and it's pro's and cons.
description: Overall, I would say it's a reliable VPN resource, flexible and rather straightforward. However there are some things that could be improved in my opinion, such as working with larger enterprises. If you have a bigger business you may want to consider a different option with more enterprise centered motivations.
author: Misty V.
pros: What i liked most about Access Server, was the user friendly interface. It's simple yet intuitive dashboard sets it off against other products. Also, for a medium sized business this would be a excellent reasonably priced VPN.
cons: The trait that I disliked the most about Access Server was, for larger institutions it can get quite complex when it comes to setup and maintenances. I would say it's perfect for small and medium sized businesses, but you should find a different option for larger institution's.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Easy to use. Automatically keeps you logged in so you dont have to log in each time you need to use systems
cons: When the VPN is down or logs you out, the access needed for certain systems we use does not allow us to log in at all and get access by our IT

review_title: Best VPN service you can get
description: It is the best VPN service that I have used so far. No other VPN service even comes close to the performance of OpenVPN.
author: Joseph M.
pros: Learning how to use the VPN is easier since the interface is very user friendly. It provides a secured workspace without compromising the Internet speed and device performance. it provides a stable server network and updates rarely cause any disruption in the service. Contacting customer support is also easy in the rare times that there are issues.
cons: I like how Open VPN works. There are just rare instances that when updates are available, it stops working but once the update is completed, everything will be back to normal.

author: Same F.
pros: SSL is very useful Encryption Standards Advanced Encryption Standard (AES) RSA (Rivest–Shamir–Adleman) Elliptic Curve Cryptography (ECC) Key Management Public Key Infrastructure (PKI) Key Exchange Protocols (e.g., Diffie-Hellman, ECDH) Certificate Authority (CA)
cons: Easy to use Protocols Secure Sockets Layer (SSL) Transport Layer Security (TLS) Datagram Transport Layer Security (DTLS) VPN Configuration Tunnel Mode vs Transport Mode Split Tunneling Mutual Authentication

description: Overall amazing no issues whatsoever. Was great for a higher education campus to allow staff, faculty, and members secure connection and access to private network resources
author: Sebastian C.
pros: Good simple vpn for a secure network. Helpful customer support to keep performance fast. Server network was strong and provided good value for money. No learning curve present and was very easy to update
cons: Maybe slightly outdated UI but overall not bad. Also maybe lacking a few features when considered multiple networks.

review_title: Probably my favourite enterprise VPN solition
description: I first used OpenVPN at a company in 2021, I enjoyed managing it so much and found it so easy to use yet fit for purpose and importantly, secure, that I've implemented it at all 4 companies I have worked at since then, including my current one now. From how easy it is to get started, how customisable it is, how reliable and robust it is, how fair priced it is to how good the support team are I keep going back to it for a reason.
author: Rian S.
pros: Other than offering a free plan, I love how quickly and user friendly it is to get started with the setup. From signing up to downloading and installing the appliance the entire thing can be done and up and running in basic form in around 20/30 minutes.
cons: Purely because I am struggling to think of anything else the Azure SAML SSO is a bit clunky. It requires the user to sign in via a web browser, which is usual, but then there's a lingering tab which is supposed to automatically close after 5 seconds but often doesn't, or there is a message within the OpenVPN app that says an error occurred and prompts the user to retry but it doesn't actually fail, it works. But these are minuscule issues that don't stop us from using the app or impact performance.

review_title: The most amazing thing to happen to cyber security!
description: I love it one of the best cyber security products ever.
author: Kerby R.
pros: It's deployable on every cloud platform. So you can always have a few servers to pick from.
cons: I wish they had an auto setup like outline. Sometimes you just don't have the time.

description: We have at least today 350 users using this software at home. In this pandemic world was the more important tool we use.
author: Emiliano Ariel V.
pros: Good performance and ease to use before a few months
cons: Not so easy for install. There is a lot of documents about this but still a barrier to enter.

author: Michal P.
pros: Openvpn works pretty well accross a number of platforms, and lets me connect to my network. The system allows for many concurrent connections and performs well.
cons: The set up and implementation of the server was difficult at first. It took a bit of tries to have it working properly. Once properly configured it was easier to manage however.

description: very easy to user, the GUI is user's friendly
author: Raymond D.
pros: it is very to use, and you will ensure a secure browsing
cons: with my 6 months of use, none so far that I don't like with this VPN
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Free, Easy and Secure VPN Solution
description: Our customers are happy because everyone likes a free product which is secure and is easy to implement (hence low labor costs).
author: Abhijeet J.
pros: OpenVPN is completely free, has a good support community online (has a big user base which helps tremendously for any opensource software) and is easy to implement for both Windows and Linux environment.
cons: Has speed limitations (due to protocol being used) so it cannot be used for high bandwidth scenarios. Key distribution is a problem too like with many other solutions out there.

review_title: OpenVPN - Simple, minimal VPN client for large teams
description: OpenVPN Connect is super easy to use. A simple toggle turns the VPN on or off. My company uses it to have double-layer encryption for all of our services while we work remotely. The only notifications are for necessary notifications, no annoying pop-ups, upsells, etc. Very minimal, clean interface. Straight to the point which is exactly what me and my team need. Excellent piece of software!
author: Erik M.
pros: OpenVPN Connect is super easy to use. A simple toggle turns the VPN on or off. My company uses it to have double-layer encryption for all of our services while we work remotely. The only notifications are for necessary notifications, no annoying pop-ups, upsells, etc. Very minimal, clean interface. Straight to the point which is exactly what me and my team need. Excellent piece of software!
cons: I can't think of anything I don't like about OpenVPN Connect. The only notifications are for necessary notifications, no annoying pop-ups, upsells, etc. Very minimal, clean interface. Straight to the point which is exactly what me and my team need.

description: I use OpenVPN to access my company's network when I am working remotely. This keeps me productive no matter where I am. It works great and I have literally never had an issue with it.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: I have used many other VPN clients before and OpenVPN is my favorite. All clients do a fine job of getting me VPN access when I need it, but OpenVPN keeps me connected while my laptop is off or asleep and it remembers my login credentials to make signing back in very quick.
cons: I really have nothing to complain about. OpenVPN is a very simple and useful VPN client. I have used it for a while with zero issues.

review_title: OpenVPN / Router Client is a Viable Choice for Privacy and Security
description: I have found OpenVPN when implemented at the router level to be an effective means of protecting one's internet activities from intruders and bad actors. Performance could be better.
author: Karambir K.
pros: I like that I can setup OpenVPN at the router level for my organization and not have to install a VPN client on each device.
cons: OpenVPN degrades performance noticeably (i.e., about 66%) compared to unprotected internet access.

author: jamieal J.
pros: software is great make everything surfing wise better for those having trouble with linking there most loved sites
cons: software is great and exceeds expectations so i recommend this software

author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Really easy to use, and super compact. You can connect and create a vpn server really quickly.
cons: Requires a lot of knowledge to do it good and secure, and should be more explanations

review_title: The simple solution we were looking for
description: Great way to quickly get a team connected to our cloud VPN server. This was the infrastructure integration piece we needed for a smooth rollout. We used it to grant access to our custom cyber range build within the AWS cloud.
author: Brook R.
pros: Straightforward to install and keep updated. Easy to set up multiple connection profiles. Configurable to run in the background, and provide seamless connections.
cons: Seems to be resource-intensive, even when it's not doing anything. Version updates are not automatic.

author: Verified Reviewer
pros: OpenVPN Clint is light weighted and VPN server is easy to configure.
cons: It is not compatible with some of proxy servers.

review_title: OpenVPN Access Server is a secure and fast connecting.
author: Denice J.
pros: This Virtual Private Network allows me to access the website those work inappropriately OpenVPN Access Server is the best VPN software that resolves my issues which occurs while browsing. I will get updates off n on to secure connections.
cons: I need customer support through email or chat but i am unable to contact with them, because i want to tell them about some issues. But i have no disadvantages about this software. 100% recommended.

description: Overall I have a very positive experience with OpenVPN, I have not had any technical issue or noticed any bugs since onboarding with them.
author: Jeremy K.
pros: Working with proprietary data means that we need the best security possible to access our network - OpenVPN makes this extremely easy to do this without worrying about network security when we are not in the office.
cons: Setup can be improved to be more simple but I understand the amount of security it must take to make sure it runs properly - our IT team was able to help us through this.

description: OpenVPN Access Server helped us secure our internal FedRAMP environment without the complications or cost of an enterprise solution.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: OpenVPN Access Server is a great solution for any size business that is looking for an economical and feature rich solution.
cons: I didn't find anything with OpenVPN Access Server that I didn't like. It had an easy to configure interface and the client just worked.

review_title: Easy to Use, Works Better Than Manufacturer Provided Client
description: Our OpenVPN experience has been very positive compared to it's alternatives.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: We were having issues with the manufacturer provided VPN client, but once we switched to using the OpenVPN client we have had very little issues
cons: We do not have many options, but at least it takes a config file from our provider which makes it easier to use the options.

review_title: OpenVPN Access Server - Depends On User
description: Provides excellent service especially per cost.
author: Sanel P.
pros: Easy enough for most users to understand how to set up. Pretty secure. Able to use in most settings.
cons: Users without a stronger technical background will find managing the software difficult. Can be even more complicated in enterprise environments. In event of reconfiguration, a user of stronger technical background is needed.
